frame_analyses.description: Needle driver grasping needle on orange practice block. Two needle drivers visible, positioned for suture passage.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Needle grasp appears to be at mid-shaft; instrument handling posture neutral.

frame_analyses.description: Both needle drivers holding needle and suture. Right hand driver controlling needle shaft.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Needle orientation appropriate for forward drive through tissue equivalent.

frame_analyses.description: Needle being driven through practice block. Entry point visible on the marked training material.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Needle trajectory appears perpendicular to target surface. Entry mark alignment looks reasonable.

frame_analyses.description: Needle emerging from exit side of block. Second mark visible on block surface.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Exit point appears aligned with marked target. Suture following needle path cleanly.

frame_analyses.description: Suture being held taut. Right hand driver holding long end of suture, preparing for first knot wrap.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Suture length and positioning appropriate for surgeon's knot formation. Right hand will lead first throw.

frame_analyses.description: Right hand driver wrapping suture around left driver tip in first wrap of surgeon's knot.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean wrap observed. Double throw formation in progress—right hand leading as expected.

frame_analyses.description: First throw (surgeon's knot double wrap) being cinched. Both drivers applying tension.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Knot appears to set cleanly. Tension applied symmetrically. Double throw confirmed visually.

frame_analyses.description: Left hand driver now leading the wrap for second throw. Hand switch observed from first throw.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper hand alternation: right led first throw; left now leads second. Single wrap formation.

frame_analyses.description: Second throw being cinched. Left hand tension control visible.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean single throw knot. Appropriate tension applied. Knot security appears good.

frame_analyses.description: Right hand driver again leading the wrap for third throw. Hand switch from second throw confirmed.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper alternation pattern maintained: left led second; right now leads third. Single wrap formation.

frame_analyses.description: Third throw being cinched with right hand leading tension.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean knot formation. Tension appropriate. All three throws now complete.

frame_analyses.description: Laparoscopic scissors positioned at suture tails. Both suture ends ready for cutting.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Scissors grasp appropriate. Both ends visible and accessible for cutting.

frame_analyses.description: One suture end being cut with scissors.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Cut appears clean at appropriate distance from knot.

frame_analyses.description: Final suture end being cut. Both ends now sectioned.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Task completion evident. Both suture tails cut.

penalties.description: Entry point approximately 1.0 mm lateral to first marked entry point; exit point approximately 1.5 mm from second mark. Total cumulative deviation estimated at 2.5 mm.

knot_assessments.notes: Clean double throw with appropriate wrap tension. Knot sits well on suture line.
Proper hand alternation from throw 1. Single wrap clean and secure.
Hand switch correctly executed. Final knot appears stable and well-seated.

confidence_rationale: Completion time well-observed and verified across frame sequence. All three throws clearly visible and hand alternation pattern confirmed. Suture deviation estimated from oblique camera angles on entry/exit points—exact millimeter measurements moderately uncertain due to block angle variation. Knot quality and security assessments straightforward based on visual inspection. Overall performance trajectory clear despite moderate uncertainty on precise entry deviation.
cannot_determine: Exact mm deviation at entry and exit marks—camera angle is not perpendicular to block surface at all frames, introducing ~±0.5mm estimation uncertainty
technique_summary: Fast, proficient expert-level performance. Completion time 92 seconds is well below the 112-second proficiency benchmark and squarely in the expert range (80–120s). All three required knots (surgeon's knot plus two single throws) executed cleanly with correct hand alternation throughout. Suture placement appears accurate with minimal deviation. No drain damage, no knot slippage, no gaps visible.
strengths: Rapid needle loading and suture passage (6-12s combined, expert level)
Clean double throw for surgeon's knot—good wrap geometry and tension control
Consistent and reliable hand switching across all three throws—proper technique demonstrated
Efficient suture cutting with both ends sectioned cleanly
Smooth overall workflow with minimal wasted motion
improvement_suggestions: Second and third throws took 18s each (vs 8–15s expert benchmark); consider accelerating cinching speed on single throws while maintaining knot security
Suture cut phase (24s) could be reduced to <12s by pre-positioning scissors earlier
